title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox won 3-0 against the Reds, utilizing MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System effectively.
Roman Anthony's successful ABS challenge in the ninth inning led to a crucial walk, extending Boston's lead.
Garrett Crochet pitched six shutout innings, contributing to Boston's Opening Day victory over Cincinnati.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer impressed on Opening Day with two hits as a pinch-hitter, helping the Red Sox defeat the Reds 3-0.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora held Mayer out of the starting lineup due to past offensive struggles and injury concerns.
Mayer entered the game in the seventh inning and contributed two runs, aiding Boston's victory over Cincinnati.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog suffered a severe leg injury from a solar light while his owners were away on vacation.
Campton-Thornton firefighters applied a tourniquet, controlling bleeding, and took Ziggy to an animal hospital for surgery.
The veterinarian confirmed the firefighters' prompt actions saved Ziggy's life, allowing for his full recovery.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy is consuming excessive oil, with the owner advised to replace the short block.
AAA's Car Doctor suggests checking oil control piston rings and PCV system for elevated oil consumption.
Subaru quoted approximately $4,000 for the short block replacement, a significant investment per AAA's analysis.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner on Nantucket sold its fifth lottery ticket worth over $1 million since March 2024.
This month, Old South Diner sold two $2 million winning tickets, according to state lottery officials.
The diner receives a $20,000 bonus for each winning ticket sold, reported the Mass. State Lottery Commission.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, a former Boston school bus driver, was arraigned on charges related to the death of 5-year-old Lens Arthur Joseph.
Months after the fatal crash, Charles was allegedly involved in a hit-and-run in Brockton, reportedly driving with a suspended license.
Charles' school bus certificate had expired, and he reportedly neglected required safety inspections before the fatal incident.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet struck out eight over six scoreless innings, leading the Red Sox to a 3-0 win over the Reds.
Marcelo Mayer's pinch-hit double in the seventh inning contributed to the Red Sox's victory on Opening Day.
Boston's bullpen, with standout performances from Justin Slaten, Garrett Whitlock, and Aroldis Chapman, secured the shutout win.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, 57, found dead in Crystal Lake, Gardner nearly five months after disappearing from Heywood Hospital.
Mercury went missing on October 31, escaping the hospital in distress by breaking a window and had no shoes.
Gardner police, using drones, found Mercury's body; State Police Dive Team retrieved it; cause of death pending.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, from Peabody, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming a femur tumor diagnosed in 2014.
Surgery at Mass General Hospital and over a decade of low-impact activity helped Southern regain running capability.
Southern is running the marathon to support Mass General Hospital, crediting it for restoring her mobility and confidence.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz, 39, of Cambridge will run her fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General Hospital patients.
Goetz, who trained in pediatrics and psychiatry, has been inspired by her experiences with young cancer patients.
She credits running with helping her overcome depression and is dedicated to raising funds for MGH through her marathon efforts.
